name: Hammin Di Pesach (Lamb, Meatballs & Spinach for Passover)
recipeIngredient: 2 1/2 pounds Lamb, breast of or rib chops
2 pounds Spinach, cooked and slightly drained
4 Matzot, regular, broken into large pieces
recipeInstructions:
Matza meal and Matzots may be purchased in some supermarkets and Jewish grocery stores.
(The word Hammin (or Hammim) is derived from the Hebrew adjective Ham or warm which describes certain dishes that are kept warm for prolonged periods of time... In Pitigliano, however Hammin was the classic one-course meal for Passover that both rich and poor used to make. The only difference between the versions was the in the cuts of meat used. Poor people used breast of lamb..and meatballs made with beef (when the cost of ground beef was a fraction of the cost of chicken.) People who didn't have to worry about the cost used tiny rib chops from baby lamb and chicken balls."
1. Combine ground chicken, 2 tablespoons olive oil, egg, matza meal, and 2 tablespoons broth in a small bowl. Add salt, pepper, and nutmeg to taste and mix well.
2. In a large pot, place the lamb with the remaining oil, sprinkle with 1 teaspoon salt and 1/4 teaspoon pepper, and lightly brown for 2-3 minutes.
3. Add garlic, parsley, and 1 cup warm water. Form small portions of the ground chicken mixture and gently add them to the pot with the lamb.
4. Cover and simmer for 1 to 1 1/2 hours. Add spinach and 1 teaspoon salt, then simmer, covered, for an additional 15 minutes longer.
5. Transfer the lamb chops, half the spinach, and most of the gravy to a hot serving dish and keep warm.
6. Add remaining broth to the pot and bring to a boil. Add matza pieces and cook for 4 to 5 minutes.
7. Turn off the heat and let stand for 5 minutes before serving as a soup. Serve the lamb and spinach as the main dish. SERVES: 6-8
